title: Equity-appropriate transaction-cost model: per-ticker spread/slippage instead of flat 10bps
source: 2026-07-03 deep project review (alpha-readiness audit); gaps previously lived only in spec prose
why: equity_d1.yaml has no friction block; resolve_cost_bps falls back to a flat 10bps for every name, and run_backtest.py still hardcodes resolve_cost_bps('XAUUSD', cfg) at two call sites (harmless today only because it hits the flat fallback). Flat 10bps is defensible for a mega-cap-only universe (conservative) but mis-calibrated in opposite directions for ETFs (~1-2bps real) vs any less-liquid tail — it distorts the ETF control group of B0003 and blocks any future down-cap extension.
value_rationale: Medium: first M3 test survives with flat costs on mega-caps; required for the ETF control to be interpretable and for universe broadening.
scope: Fix XAUUSD hardcode in run_backtest.py -> pass the actual asset/ticker
Per-ticker cost resolution (spread estimate e.g. Corwin-Schultz already computed, or static per-liquidity-bucket bps) via friction config
Separate ETF cost tier for the B0003 control run
